seo.title: OpenAI Assistants API Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for OpenAI Assistants API agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams using OpenAI Assistants API face heightened compliance risks due to dynamic customer interactions involving product recommendations, pricing, and policy explanations. Without guardrails, Assistants can hallucinate unsubstantiated claims, misrepresent pricing, or leak sensitive payment data, violating FTC guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS, and ADA. Vex provides automated verification on run completion to ensure responses align with regulations and business policies.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims must be substantiated with evidence; Assistants cannot make unverified health, performance, or efficacy statements.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies claims against product catalogs and marketing approvals using semantic search and schema validation.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer data handling must include opt-out rights and no unnecessary PII exposure in responses.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans Assistant outputs for PII leakage and ensures privacy policy links are included where required.

complianceRequirements.description: No cardholder data or payment details can be processed or stored in Assistant threads.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex blocks PCI-sensitive patterns in function calls and thread context using regex and NER detection.

complianceRequirements.description: Product descriptions and checkout flows must be accessible with alt text and clear language.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex checks generated content for WCAG compliance including color contrast and semantic structure.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated Pricing
industryRisks.description: Assistant invents discounts or prices not matching inventory system.
industryRisks.example: Customer sees '50% off' that doesn't exist, leading to refund disputes and chargebacks.

industryRisks.name: Unsubstantiated Claims
industryRisks.description: Assistant makes unapproved product benefit statements.
industryRisks.example: 'Cures diabetes' claim triggers FTC investigation and fines.

industryRisks.name: PII Exposure in Threads
industryRisks.description: Customer email/phone stored in growing thread context gets exposed.
industryRisks.example: GDPR violation from leaked customer data in shared debug logs.

industryRisks.name: Inaccurate Refund Policy
industryRisks.description: Assistant misrepresents return windows or conditions.
industryRisks.example: Customer returns item after '90-day' policy hallucinated (actual 30 days), causing support overload.

implementation.title: Webhook for Run Completion Verification
implementation.description: Set up Vex webhook to validate Assistant responses against e-commerce policies on every run completion.

implementation.title: TypeScript Webhook Handler
implementation.description: Express.js endpoint to receive OpenAI run completion webhooks and verify with Vex.

implementation.title: Python Policy Definition for E-Commerce
implementation.description: Define custom Vex policies for pricing accuracy and FTC compliance checks.
implementation.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient, Policy

vex = VexClient(api_key='vex-key')

policy = Policy(name='ecommerce-strict').add_checks([
    {
        'name': 'pricing_accuracy',
        'type': 'regex_schema',
        'pattern': r'\$(\d+\.\d{2})',
        'require_evidence': 'must_match_catalog',
        'fail_message': 'Pricing must match current catalog'
    },
    {
        'name': 'ftc_claims',
        'type': 'semantic_search',
        'documents': ['approved-claims.json'],
        'threshold': 0.9,
        'fail_message': 'Unsubstantiated product claims detected'
    },
    {
        'name': 'pci_block',
        'type': 'pii_detection',
        'patterns': ['\d{4}-\d{4}-\d{4}-\d{4}', '\\b4[0-9]{12}(?:[0-9]{3})?\\b'],
        'action': 'block'
    },
    {
        'name': 'return_policy',
        'type': 'exact_match',
        'required_phrases': ['30-day return', 'full refund if unopened'],
        'fail_message': 'Must include accurate return policy'
    }
])

policy.deploy()

caseStudyScenario.heading: OpenAI Assistants API in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
FashionCo deployed OpenAI Assistants API to handle customer service across their website, mobile app, and WhatsApp integration. The Assistant was equipped with file search over 10,000 product SKUs, code interpreter for inventory calculations, and function calling to their Shopify backend. It fielded 5,000 daily queries about sizing, pricing, shipping, and returns. Initially, the system generated impressive response times but began showing issues: hallucinated 'buy one get one free' promotions during sales, inconsistent return policy statements (claiming 90 days vs actual 30), and occasional exposure of customer email addresses in thread debugging.

Marketing teams noticed FTC-risky claims like 'medically approved compression socks' without substantiation. Pricing errors led to 300+ refund disputes monthly when the code interpreter miscalculated bundle discounts. Thread context ballooned beyond 128k tokens, causing silent truncation of order history and accessibility issues in generated alt text for product images.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex verification as a run completion webhook, FashionCo achieved 99.7% compliance across all interactions. Pricing hallucinations dropped to zero with schema-enforced catalog matching. FTC claims were automatically blocked and flagged for review, reducing risk exposure by 95%. PCI-sensitive data flows were caught before reaching persistent storage. Customer satisfaction improved 22% due to consistent, accurate policy representation, and refund disputes fell 87%. The Vex dashboard provided real-time compliance monitoring, enabling proactive policy updates during flash sales.

cta.heading: Comply with E-Commerce Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex verification to your OpenAI Assistants today and eliminate compliance risks across all customer touchpoints.
